title: Sweet orange sugar scrub
subtitle: A body scrub with granulated sugar, sweet almond oil, and sweet orange essential oil
excerpt: A simple body scrub made from granulated sugar and sweet almond oil, scented with sweet orange essential oil. The sugar exfoliates while the oil moisturises the skin as the scrub rinses away. Makes enough for 5 to 6 shower uses. Contains tree nut allergen from sweet almond oil.
sourceNotes: Tisserand and Young, Essential Oil Safety (2nd ed., 2014): sweet orange (cold-pressed) classified as non-phototoxic at standard use rates. Formulators Kitchen rinse-off product EO rates (formulatorskitchen.com, accessed May 2026).

content.text: : it is applied to the skin, massaged in briefly, and washed away with water. The granulated sugar dissolves gradually during application, exfoliating dead skin cells as it does so. The sweet almond oil is left as a thin film on the skin after rinsing, which reduces the need for additional moisturiser.
Sweet orange essential oil is cold-pressed from the peel of the orange fruit and is not

at standard use rates, which distinguishes it from bergamot and bitter orange. Do not substitute bitter orange peel oil without checking phototoxicity guidance. This scrub contains sweet almond oil, which contains tree nut allergens; label clearly if gifting.

items.name: Granulated sugar
items.prepNote: Standard granulated white sugar. The grain size is suitable for body use. Do not use icing sugar, which dissolves too quickly, or coarse rock sugar, which is too harsh.

items.name: Sweet almond oil
items.prepNote: Light, absorbed easily into wet skin. Contains tree nut allergens. Substitute jojoba oil for a nut-free version.

items.name: Sweet orange essential oil
items.prepNote: 2.3% of total product weight. Cold-pressed sweet orange peel oil. Non-phototoxic at this dilution.

content.text: Weigh the granulated sugar into the mixing bowl. Add the sweet almond oil and sweet orange essential oil. Stir until the sugar is evenly coated. The mixture will look like wet sand. Spoon into a clean, dry glass jar with a wide mouth. Seal and label with the making date and tree nut content.

Use a dry spoon to scoop 2 to 3 tablespoons of scrub from the jar. Apply to damp skin in the shower, massage in circular motions for 30 to 60 seconds per area, then rinse thoroughly. The skin will feel oiled after rinsing, which is normal. Take care in the shower after use; the oil makes the floor slippery.

Keep in a sealed glass jar in a dry place, not in the shower where steam and water droplets will enter the jar. Always use a dry spoon to scoop; wet hands introduce water and can cause mould. The scrub keeps for 12 months. The oil and sugar separate on standing; stir before each use. Discard if the oil smells rancid or any mould appears.
